# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

FROM docker:24-dind

RUN apk add --no-cache \
    bash \
    curl \
    jq \
    python3 \
    py3-pip

RUN addgroup -S orchestrator && \
    adduser -S -G orchestrator -h /app orchestrator

RUN addgroup orchestrator docker 2>/dev/null || true

WORKDIR /app

COPY --chown=orchestrator:orchestrator orchestration/ ./orchestration/
COPY --chown=orchestrator:orchestrator entrypoint.sh ./

RUN chmod +x entrypoint.sh

# Drop all capabilities by default; the container runtime should grant
# only the specific capabilities needed (e.g., via --cap-add) rather than
# running as privileged. Do NOT use --privileged at runtime.
# The Docker socket should be mounted read-only if write access is not
# strictly required, or scoped via a socket proxy.

# Run as the non-root orchestrator user. The user is in the docker group
# which grants access to the Docker socket when it is bind-mounted.
USER orchestrator

# Health check to verify the service and Docker socket connectivity
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD docker info > /dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1

# Document that the Docker socket must be mounted at runtime:
#   docker run -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro \
#     --cap-drop=ALL --cap-add=NET_BIND_SERVICE \
#     --security-opt=no-new-privileges:true \
#     orchestrator-image
LABEL maintainer="orchestration-team" \
      description="Container orchestration service - requires Docker socket mount" \
      security.note="Do NOT run with --privileged. Mount Docker socket and add only required capabilities."

ENTRYPOINT ["./entrypoint.sh"]
